overview: This step converts characters in text units from or to full-width form (zenkaku). For historical reasons, some Asian character sets have two display forms for some characters: half-width and full-width. The modification is done in the text of the text units for the specified target locale; if there is no text for the specified target, the source text is copied to the target and processed. Takes filter events and sends filter events.

toHalfWidth.description: When true, converts all full-width characters to their half-width or ASCII equivalents. For example, 'Ｑ' (U+FF31) is converted to 'Q' (U+0051) and 'サ' (U+30B5) is converted to 'ｻ' (U+FF7B). When false, converts in the opposite direction: all half-width and ASCII characters are converted to their full-width equivalents. For example, 'Q' (U+0051) is converted to 'Ｑ' (U+FF31) and 'ｻ' (U+FF7B) is converted to 'サ' (U+30B5).

asciiOnly.description: When set, converts only ASCII characters to full-width. Half-width characters are left as half-width. This option applies only when converting to full-width (toHalfWidth is false).

katakanaOnly.description: When set, converts only Japanese Katakana and associated punctuation (｡､｢｣, etc.) into their full-width forms. This is a separate option to facilitate normalizing modern Japanese text: half-width Katakana should be converted to full-width while alphanumeric characters should remain half-width. This option applies only when converting to full-width (toHalfWidth is false).

includeSLA.description: When set, also converts the Squared Latin Abbreviations of the CJK Compatibility block into sequences of non-CJK characters. For example, '㏀' (U+33C0) is converted to "kΩ" (U+006B, U+03A9). This option applies when converting to half-width.

includeLLS.description: When set, also converts several characters of the Letter-Like Symbols block to character sequences. Conversions include: U+2100→a/c, U+2101→a/s, U+2105→c/o, U+2103→°C, U+2109→°F, U+2116→No, U+212A→K, U+212B→Å. This option applies when converting to half-width.

includeKatakana.description: When set, converts Japanese Katakana and associated punctuation (。、「」, etc.) into their half-width forms. This is a separate option (and off by default) to facilitate normalizing modern Japanese text: full-width alphanumeric characters may need to be normalized to half-width while Katakana should remain full-width. This option applies when converting to half-width.

normalizeOutput.description: When enabled, applies Unicode NFC normalization to the output text if any conversions are made. Converting half-width forms to full-width can result in decomposed forms — for instance, ﾌﾟ (U+FF8C U+FF9F) → プ (U+30D5 U+309A). Normalization ensures the standard composed representation is used: プ (U+30D7).

processingNotes: The modification is done on the text of the text units for the specified target locale. If there is no text for the specified target, the source text is copied to the target and then processed.
Converting half-width forms to full-width can result in Unicode decomposed forms; enable normalizeOutput to ensure NFC-normalized output.

examples.title: Normalize Japanese text (half-width alphanumeric, full-width Katakana)
examples.description: Convert full-width alphanumeric characters to half-width while leaving Katakana in full-width form. This is a common normalization for modern Japanese text.
examples.input: toHalfWidth: true, includeKatakana: false (default), normalizeOutput: true
examples.output: Full-width ASCII/alphanumeric characters like Ｑ (U+FF31) are converted to Q (U+0051), while Katakana characters like サ (U+30B5) remain unchanged.

examples.title: Convert half-width Katakana to full-width only
examples.description: Convert half-width Katakana to full-width while leaving alphanumeric characters in their current form. Useful for normalizing Japanese text where Katakana should be full-width but ASCII should stay half-width.
examples.input: toHalfWidth: false, katakanaOnly: true, normalizeOutput: true
examples.output: Half-width Katakana like ｻ (U+FF7B) is converted to サ (U+30B5). ASCII characters remain unchanged. NFC normalization is applied to handle decomposed forms.

examples.title: Full-width to half-width with Letter-Like Symbols
examples.description: Convert full-width characters to half-width and also expand Letter-Like Symbols into character sequences.
examples.output: Full-width characters are converted to half-width equivalents, and Letter-Like Symbols such as ℃ (U+2103) are expanded to °C.
